experiment: F-STR3 wave planner prospective accuracy check
hypothesis: Wave planner recommendations (COMMIT/CLOSE) are followed >=50% and mode= adoption >=30%
method: Count explicit mode= fields in S389-S394 lanes. Track COMMIT and CLOSE recommendation follow-through against actual lanes opened. Measure mode-shift compliance.

mode_adoption.note: Voluntary adoption hit 100% by S392 BEFORE enforcement added in S393. Creation-time WARN sufficient when cost is low (single field).

commit_follow_through.s389_danger_zone_5_frontiers: 5/5 = 100% (F-IS4, F-GT1, F-PHY1, F-GUE1, F-PRO1 all received 3rd waves)

verdict: PARTIAL — adoption exceeds targets (84% vs 30%), in-pipeline COMMIT 100%, but cold-domain recommendations 0% follow-through. Wave planner diagnoses correctly but cannot dispatch.
structural_gap: Wave planner recommendations are advisory-only. UCB1 controls execution. For domains with exploit=0 (social-media, cryptocurrency), +1.5 danger boost insufficient to overcome 0.0 exploit term. Fix: inject COMMIT priorities as UCB1 floor, or accept cold domains require manual dispatch.
l601_partial_contradiction: mode= voluntary adoption sustained at 100% before structural enforcement. Creation-time WARNs with low adoption cost can sustain compliance — L-601 may have a cost threshold below which voluntary protocols persist.
prescriptions: Add UCB1 floor for COMMIT-flagged frontiers (minimum score = median domain score)
Or: flag cold-domain COMMITs in orient.py DUE section for human/manual dispatch
Track full explore->harden->resolve traversal rate as F-STR3 health metric
